sentences.base_language: It was Omar's thirty-fifth birthday and his friends had been secretly planning a surprise party for weeks.
sentences.target_language: كان ذلك عيدَ ميلاد عمر الخامسَ والثلاثين وكان أصدقاؤه يُخطِّطون سرًّا لحفلةٍ مفاجئةٍ منذ أسابيع.
sentences.romanized: Kāna dhālika ʿīda mīlādi ʿUmara l-khāmisa wa-th-thalāthīna wa-kāna aṣdiqāʾuhu yukhaṭṭiṭūna sirran li-ḥaflatin mufājiʾatin mundhu asābīʿ.
sentences.grammarNote:
**الخامسَ والثلاثين** is a compound ordinal number in Arabic: the unit ordinal (الخامس, fifth) precedes the tens (والثلاثين), both in the accusative as a noun phrase in apposition to عيد; this unit-before-tens order is standard for ordinals from 11 to 99 in MSA.
**كان يُخطِّطون** is كان + مضارع (imperfect), the past progressive construction expressing an ongoing, durative action in the past — his friends had been planning; Form II يُخطِّط (from خطَّط, to plan) conveys deliberate, methodical planning, and منذ أسابيع anchors the duration.

sentences.base_language: He spent the morning convinced it would be a quiet day — he had told everyone he didn't want any fuss.
sentences.target_language: قضى عمر الصباحَ مقتنعاً بأنه سيكون يوماً هادئاً — فقد أخبر الجميعَ أنه لا يريد أيَّ ضجَّةٍ.
sentences.romanized: Qaḍā ʿUmaru ṣ-ṣabāḥa muqtaniʿan bi-annahu sa-yakūnu yawman hādiʾan — fa-qad akhbara l-jamīʿa annahu lā yurīdu ayya ḍajjah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**مقتنعاً** is the Form VIII active participle (مُفتعِل) of قنع (اقتنع, to be convinced/persuaded), functioning as a ḥāl — in a state of being convinced; the accusative ـاً marks it as a ḥāl describing the subject عمر during the action of spending the morning.
**أيَّ ضجَّةٍ** uses the noun أيّ (any) as a مضاف in the accusative, governing ضجَّة in the genitive with tanwīn; this إضافة construction with أيّ is the standard MSA way to negate or question complete absence — he wanted no fuss whatsoever.

sentences.base_language: His closest friend called in the afternoon and casually suggested coming over for a simple dinner together.
sentences.target_language: اتَّصل به أقربُ أصدقائه في المساء واقترح بكلِّ تلقائيةٍ أن يأتي إليه لعشاءٍ بسيطٍ مشترك.
sentences.romanized: Ittaṣala bihi aqrabu aṣdiqāʾihi fī l-masāʾi wa-qtaraḥa bi-kulli talqāʾiyyatin an yaʾtiya ilayhi li-ʿashāʾin basīṭin mushtarak.
sentences.grammarNote:
**اقترح** is Form VIII of قرح (to propose/suggest); Form VIII (افتعل) here expresses a volitional, self-initiated proposal and takes أن + مضارع منصوب (subjunctive يأتي) as its complement, the standard MSA pattern for verbs of suggestion and request.
**أقربُ أصدقائه** is an elative (أفعل of تفضيل) in إضافة with the broken plural أصدقاء in the genitive; أقرب as مضاف is indefinite and in the nominative (أقربُ, subject of the inverted sentence), and the إضافة with a plural specifies the domain of comparison — closest among his friends.

sentences.base_language: When Omar arrived and the door opened, a room full of people shouted Surprise! all at once.
sentences.target_language: حين وصل عمر وانفتح البابُ، صرخ جمعٌ من الناس ملءَ الغرفة بكلمة المفاجأة دفعةً واحدة.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna waṣala ʿUmaru wa-nfataḥa l-bābu, ṣarakha jamʿun min an-nāsi milʾa l-ghurfati bi-kalimati l-mufājaʾati dafʿatan wāḥidah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**انفتح** is Form VII of فتح (to open), the reflexive-passive of Form I; Form VII (انفعل) is used for spontaneous or unagentive events — the door opened by itself (from Omar's perspective), without naming the person who opened it, creating the narrative effect of surprise.
**دفعةً واحدة** is an adverbial accusative of manner (مفعول مطلق in function), literally one surge/push — all at once; this fixed MSA expression intensifies simultaneity, and the accusative tanwīn marks دفعة as an indefinite adverbial.

sentences.base_language: His heart leaped and he felt tears spring to his eyes — he had had absolutely no idea.
sentences.target_language: قفز قلبُه وأحسَّ بالدموع تتجمَّع في عينيه — لم يكن لديه أيُّ فكرةٍ على الإطلاق.
sentences.romanized: Qafaza qalbuhu wa-aḥassa bid-dumūʿi tatajammaʿu fī ʿaynayhi — lam yakun ladayhi ayyu fikratin ʿalā l-iṭlāq.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أحسَّ بالدموع تتجمَّع** uses the perception verb أحسَّ (Form IV of حسَّ, to sense/feel) followed by the preposition بـ and a noun in the accusative with a second verb تتجمَّع in the imperfect as a supplementary predicate; this Arabic construction of verb-of-perception + بـ + noun + imperfect is the equivalent of felt [something] doing [something].
**عينيه** is the dual genitive of عين (eye) with the first-person singular possessive suffix ـه (his), giving the dual genitive form عينيه; the dual genitive/accusative ending ـيْن contracts to ـيْ before a possessive suffix, producing عَيْنَيْهِ — his two eyes.

sentences.base_language: The living room was decorated with balloons and colourful lights, and his favourite music was already playing.
sentences.target_language: كانت غرفة الجلوس مُزيَّنةً بالبالونات وأضواءٍ مُلوَّنةٍ، وكانت موسيقاه المفضَّلة تعزف بالفعل.
sentences.romanized: Kānat ghurfatu l-julūsi muzayyanatan bil-bālūnāti wa-aḍwāʾin mulawwanatin, wa-kānat mūsīqāhu l-mufaḍḍalatu taʿzifu bil-fiʿl.
sentences.grammarNote:
**مُزيَّنةً** is the Form II passive participle (مُفعَّلة) of زيَّن (to decorate/adorn) in the feminine accusative, serving as predicate خبر of كانت; the Form II pattern مُفعَّل/مُفعَّلة for passive participles is highly productive in MSA for describing states resulting from deliberate, intensive action.
**مُلوَّنةٍ** is the Form II passive participle of لوَّن (to colour/tint), indefinite in the genitive agreeing with أضواء; similarly, **مُفضَّلة** is the Form II passive participle of فضَّل (to prefer), used here as a definite attributive adjective — his preferred/favourite — showing how Form II passive participles function as ordinary adjectives in MSA.

sentences.base_language: Friends from every stage of his life were there — old schoolmates, colleagues, relatives he hadn't seen in years.
sentences.target_language: كان هناك أصدقاء من كلِّ مرحلةٍ من مراحل حياته — رفاقٌ من المدرسة وزملاءُ عملٍ وأقاربُ لم يَرَهم منذ سنين.
sentences.romanized: Kāna hunāka aṣdiqāʾu min kulli marḥalatin min marāḥili ḥayātihi — rifāqun min al-madrasati wa-zumalāʾu ʿamalin wa-aqāribu lam yarāhum mundhu sinīn.
literal: من كلِّ مرحلةٍ من مراحل حياته
from every stage of stages his-life
sentences.grammarNote:
**مراحل** is the broken plural of مرحلة (stage/phase) on the pattern مَفاعِل, a common pattern for quadriliteral nouns and triliteral nouns with a long vowel in a particular position; the genitive chain مراحل حياته is a double إضافة — مراحل governing حياة, which governs the pronoun suffix ـه.
**أقاربُ** is a diptote broken plural of قريب (close relative) on the pattern أَفاعِل; diptote nouns (الممنوع من الصرف) take no tanwīn and use ـُ for both the indefinite nominative and genitive — here the nominative ـُ after the conjunction وَ — a key morphological feature of MSA broken plurals.

sentences.base_language: Omar was overwhelmed with gratitude and went around the room embracing everyone and thanking them warmly.
sentences.target_language: اعتراه الامتنانُ فجال في أرجاء الغرفة مُعانِقاً الجميعَ وشاكراً إيَّاهم بدفءٍ.
sentences.romanized: Iʿtarāhu l-imtinānु fa-jāla fī arjāʾi l-ghurfati muʿāniqan al-jamīʿa wa-shākiran iyyāhum bi-difʾ.
sentences.grammarNote:
**اعتراه** is Form VIII of عرا (اعترى, to come over/overtake someone), an expressive verb used for emotions that seize or overwhelm a person; the verb precedes its subject الامتنانُ (gratitude) in the standard VSO order, with the object pronoun ـه referring to عمر — gratitude overtook him.
**مُعانِقاً وشاكراً** are Form III and Form I active participles respectively, both in the accusative as ḥāl clauses describing عمر's state while roaming — embracing everyone and thanking them; Form III مُعانِق (from عانق, to embrace) expresses a mutual or directed action toward others, a characteristic of the Form III pattern.

sentences.base_language: The table was covered with his favourite dishes, including a slow-cooked lamb dish his mother always made for special occasions.
sentences.target_language: كانت المائدة مليئةً بأطباقه المفضَّلة، من بينها طبقُ لحمٍ مطبوخٍ على نارٍ هادئةٍ كانت أمُّه تُعدُّه دائماً للمناسبات الخاصة.
sentences.romanized: Kānat al-māʾidatu malīʾatan bi-aṭbāqihi l-mufaḍḍalati, min baynihā ṭabaqu laḥmin maṭbūkhin ʿalā nārin hādiʾatin kānat ummuhu tuʿidduhu dāʾiman lil-munāsabāti l-khāṣṣah.
literal: طبقُ لحمٍ مطبوخٍ على نارٍ هادئةٍ
dish meat cooked on fire gentle
sentences.grammarNote:
**كانت أمُّه تُعدُّه دائماً** is كان + مضارع, the past habitual construction — his mother always used to prepare it; the imperfect تُعدُّ (Form IV of أعدَّ, to prepare) within the كان يفعل frame expresses a repeated, habitual past action, contrasting with كان + قد + ماضٍ for past perfect.
**على نارٍ هادئةٍ** is the standard MSA idiom for slow cooking, literally over a gentle fire; both نارٍ and هادئةٍ carry double tanwīn as indefinite genitive nouns governed by the preposition على, showing full case marking.

sentences.base_language: Someone poured glasses of fresh juice and everyone raised a toast to Omar's health and happiness.
sentences.target_language: صبَّ أحدُهم كؤوساً من العصير الطازج ورفع الجميعُ نخبَ صحة عمر وسعادته.
sentences.romanized: Ṣabba aḥaduhum kuʾūsan min al-ʿaṣīri ṭ-ṭāzaji wa-rafaʿa l-jamīʿu nakhba ṣiḥḥati ʿUmara wa-saʿādatih.
sentences.grammarNote:
**كؤوساً** is the broken plural of كأس (cup/glass) on the pattern فُعُول; this pattern is one of the most productive in Arabic for vessel and container nouns, and the indefinite accusative tanwīn marks it as the direct object of صبَّ.
**نخبَ صحة عمر** is a double إضافة in the accusative: نخب (toast) governs صحة in the genitive, and صحة governs the proper noun عمر; رفع النخب (to raise a toast) is the standard MSA expression for toasting someone, and the omission of the definite article on نخب in this phrase shows it functioning as a semi-technical term.

sentences.base_language: Omar gave a short, heartfelt speech and tried to express how much everyone in the room meant to him.
sentences.target_language: ألقى عمر كلمةً قصيرةً من القلب وحاول أن يُعبِّر عن مدى ما يعنيه كلُّ من في الغرفة له.
sentences.romanized: Alqā ʿUmaru kalimatan qaṣīratan min al-qalbi wa-ḥāwala an yuʿabbira ʿan madā mā yaʿnīhi kullu man fī l-ghurfati lah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**يُعبِّر** is Form II of عبَّر (to express/convey), taking the preposition عن; Form II here is intensive, conveying a deliberate, considered act of expression rather than casual speech, and the subjunctive after حاول أن is standard MSA for volitional verb complements.
**مدى ما يعنيه** uses the noun مدى (extent/degree) followed by the relative pronoun ما and the verb يعنيه (Form I of عنى, to mean/signify) with the resumptive pronoun ـه; this embedded question construction — how much... means to him — is a formal MSA way to embed degree clauses.

sentences.base_language: As the evening went on, someone turned the music louder and the living room became an impromptu dance floor.
sentences.target_language: مع مضيِّ السهرة، رفع أحدُهم صوتَ الموسيقى وأصبحت غرفة الجلوس حلبةَ رقصٍ ارتجاليةً.
sentences.romanized: Maʿa muḍiyyi s-sahrati, rafaʿa aḥaduhum ṣawta l-mūsīqā wa-aṣbaḥat ghurfatu l-julūsi ḥalbata raqṣin irtijāliyyah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**مع مضيِّ السهرة** uses the masdar مضيّ (Form I verbal noun of مضى, to pass/elapse) in إضافة with السهرة; this مع + masdar + إضافة construction is the formal MSA equivalent of as the evening went on — a temporal clause compressed into a prepositional phrase with a verbal noun.
**أصبحت** is the verb أصبح (one of the كان sisters), a nominal-sentence verb meaning to become; like كان, it takes an accusative predicate — here حلبةَ رقصٍ ارتجاليةً — and places the subject in the nominative; أصبح is preferred over صار in more formal registers.

sentences.base_language: His feet and back began to ache from dancing but he couldn't bring himself to stop.
sentences.target_language: بدأت قدماه وظهرُه يؤلمانه من فرط الرقص، غير أنه ما كان في مقدوره أن يتوقَّف.
sentences.romanized: Badaʾat qadāmahu wa-ẓahruhu yuʾlimānihi min farṭi r-raqṣi, ghayra annahu mā kāna fī maqdūrihi an yatawwaqqaf.
sentences.grammarNote:
**قدماه** is the dual nominative of قدم (foot) with the third-person masculine singular suffix ـه (his), giving قَدَمَاهُ; the dual verb يؤلمانه takes the dual suffix ـانِ in agreement with the dual subject قدماه وظهره (coordinated as a conceptual pair), showing dual verb-subject agreement.
**ما كان في مقدوره أن يتوقَّف** uses the negation ما + كان + في + masdar-in-إضافة construction to express inability: literally it was not within his power; this formal MSA idiom for inability is more emphatic than the simple لم يستطع, and the subjunctive يتوقَّف after أن is required.

sentences.base_language: When the birthday cake appeared — a beautiful cake blazing with candles — everyone began to sing.
sentences.target_language: حين ظهرت كعكة عيد الميلاد — كعكةٌ رائعةٌ تتَّقد بالشموع — بدأ الجميعُ في الغناء.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna ẓaharat kaʿkatu ʿīdi l-mīlādi — kaʿkatun rāʾiʿatun tattaqidu biʾsh-shumuʿi — badaʾa l-jamīʿu fī l-ghinā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تتَّقد** is Form VIII of وقد (اتَّقد, to blaze/catch fire), used here as an asyndetic relative clause modifying كعكةٌ; because the head noun is indefinite (كعكةٌ), the relative clause is introduced without a relative pronoun — this zero-relative construction with indefinite antecedents is obligatory in MSA.
**بدأ في الغناء** uses the verb بدأ (to begin) with the preposition في followed by the masdar الغناء (singing); this بدأ + في + masdar construction is the standard MSA complement pattern for بدأ, distinct from بدأ + أن + مضارع which is also possible but less idiomatic with activity nouns.

sentences.base_language: Omar closed his eyes, made a silent wish, and blew out all the candles in one breath.
sentences.target_language: أغمض عمر عينيه وتمنَّى أُمنيةً صامتةً ثم أطفأ جميعَ الشموع بنفَسٍ واحد.
sentences.romanized: Aghmada ʿUmaru ʿaynayhi wa-tammannā umniyyatan ṣāmitatan thumma aṭfaʾa jamīʿa sh-shumuʿi bi-nafasin wāḥid.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أغمض** is Form IV of غمض (أغمض, to close the eyes), a causative-transitive form; Form IV makes the intransitive غمضت عيناه (his eyes closed) into a transitive action where the subject actively closes his own eyes — أغمض عينيه — a common reflexive use of Form IV causatives.
**تمنَّى أُمنيةً** is the verb تمنَّى (Form V of منى, to wish/desire) with a cognate accusative (مفعول مطلق) أُمنيةً — literally wished a wish; this figure etymologica, where the verb and its direct object share the same root, is a classical rhetorical device (توكيد) that reinforces the sincerity and depth of the action.

sentences.base_language: The cake was extraordinary — rich and soft, with a cream that tasted like something from childhood.
sentences.target_language: كانت الكعكة استثنائيةً — غنيةً وطريةً، بكريمةٍ طعمها يُذكِّر بشيءٍ من الطفولة.
sentences.romanized: Kānat al-kaʿkatu istithnaʾiyyatan — ghaniyyatan wa-ṭariyyatan, bi-krīmatin ṭaʿmuhā yudhakkiru bi-shayʾin min aṭ-ṭufūlah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**يُذكِّر** is Form II of ذكَّر (to remind/cause to remember), taking the preposition بـ; Form II here is the causative of the root ذ-ك-ر — to cause someone to remember — and the implicit object is the experiencer (عمر), elided as understood from context.
**بكريمةٍ طعمها يُذكِّر** is an indefinite noun كريمة modified by a nominal relative clause طعمها يُذكِّر; because the head noun is indefinite, the relative clause is asyndetic (no relative pronoun), and طعمها functions as the subject of the embedded clause with يُذكِّر as its predicate — a cream whose taste reminds [one].

sentences.base_language: Long after midnight, the last guests began saying their goodbyes with warm embraces at the door.
sentences.target_language: بعد منتصف الليل بوقتٍ طويلٍ، بدأ آخرُ الضيوف بالوداع وتبادل العناق الدافئ عند الباب.
sentences.romanized: Baʿda muntaṣafi l-layli bi-waqtin ṭawīlin, badaʾa ākhiru ḍ-ḍuyūfi bil-wadāʿi wa-tabāduli l-ʿināqi d-dāfiʾi ʿinda l-bāb.
sentences.grammarNote:
**بعد منتصف الليل بوقتٍ طويلٍ** is a temporal phrase using the construction بعد + noun + بـ + noun of interval; this بعد... بـ... construction, where بـ introduces the measure of time elapsed after the reference point, is a standard MSA pattern for long after — literally after midnight by a long time.
**تبادل** is Form VI of بادل (تبادل, to exchange with each other), the reciprocal pattern; Form VI (تفاعل) inherently expresses mutual action between two or more parties — the guests exchanging embraces with one another — without needing to specify both parties explicitly.

sentences.base_language: When everyone had finally left, Omar stood alone in the quiet room, still glowing with happiness.
sentences.target_language: حين غادر الجميعُ أخيراً، وقف عمر وحيداً في الغرفة الهادئة، لا يزال يفيض بهجةً وسعادةً.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna ghādara l-jamīʿu akhīran, waqafa ʿUmaru waḥīdan fī l-ghurfati l-hādiʾati, lā yazālu yafīḍu bahjatan wa-saʿādah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**لا يزال يفيض** uses the sister-of-كان verb لا يزال (to still be doing something, literally not to cease) followed by the imperfect يفيض (from فاض, to overflow/brim); this لا يزال + مضارع construction is the standard MSA form for still doing / continuing to do, expressing persistence of a state into the present narrative moment.
**بهجةً وسعادةً** are accusative nouns functioning as a tamyīz (تمييز) or adverbial complement after يفيض — overflowing with joy and happiness; the indefinite accusative tanwīn on both nouns marks them as complements of degree/content, specifying what the overflowing consists of.

sentences.base_language: He sent a long voice message to his closest friend, thanking her for organizing everything so beautifully.
sentences.target_language: أرسل رسالةً صوتيةً طويلةً إلى أقرب أصدقائه شاكراً إيَّاها على تنظيمها كلَّ شيءٍ بتلك الجمالية الرائعة.
sentences.romanized: Arsala risālatan ṣawtiyyatan ṭawīlatan ilā aqrabi aṣdiqāʾihi shākiran iyyāhā ʿalā tanẓīmihā kulla shayʾin bi-tilka l-jamāliyyati r-rāʾiʿah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**شاكراً إيَّاها** uses the active participle شاكر as a ḥāl in the accusative, with the separated pronoun إيَّاها as its object; in MSA, when a participle or verb takes a pronoun object and the subject is also pronominal, the separated pronoun form إيَّا + suffix is required rather than an attached suffix — شاكراً إيَّاها, not شاكرها.
**تنظيمها** is the masdar of Form II نظَّم (تنظيم, organization/arranging) with the feminine possessive suffix ـها; the masdar in إضافة with a pronoun suffix retains the verbal relationship — her organizing (of everything) — making تنظيمها كلَّ شيءٍ a nominalized clause where كلَّ شيءٍ is the logical object of the masdar.

sentences.base_language: Lying in bed that night, he thought this was exactly the kind of evening that stays with you for the rest of your life.
sentences.target_language: وهو مضطجعٌ في سريره تلك الليلة، خطر بباله أن هذا تماماً هو النوعُ من السهرات الذي يظلُّ معك طوال ما تبقَّى من عمرك.
sentences.romanized: Wa-huwa muḍṭajiʿun fī sarīrihi tilka l-laylata, khaṭara bi-bālihi anna hādhā tamāman huwa n-nawʿu min as-saharāti lladhī yaẓallu maʿaka ṭawāla mā tabaqqa min ʿumurik.
literal: that this exactly it is the-kind
معك طوال ما تبقَّى من عمرك
with-you throughout what remains of your-life
sentences.grammarNote:
**وهو مضطجعٌ** is a ḥāl clause introduced by the wāw al-ḥāl (وَ + pronoun), with مضطجع as the active participle of Form VIII اضطجع (to lie down, recline); this وَ + ḍamīr + participle/sentence construction is the canonical MSA way to introduce a ḥāl clause — while he was lying — specifying the attendant circumstance of the main verb خطر.
**طوال ما تبقَّى من عمرك** uses the adverb طوال (throughout/for the whole of) governing a relative clause ما تبقَّى — what remains; تبقَّى is Form V of بقي (to remain/be left), and the entire phrase طوال ما تبقَّى من عمرك is a formal MSA expression for for the rest of one's life, using the masdar-relative construction to express indefinite ongoing duration.
